            1. MeinongsJungleBook on Chapter 17 Sun 10 May 2026 09:37AM AEST

              No but that’s it exactly.

              My headcanon is that her attraction is partly based on the fact that Syril openly and embarrassingly expresses the thoughts and feelings Dedra represses (like when he said getting praised by Partagaz was the best day of his life), which allows her to feel some of the emotions she’s cut herself off from vicariously, which is cathartic for her. She uses him to have an emotional life by proxy.

              That plus the fact that he registers to her as like a version of herself without all the defensive layers triggers a protective impulse in her.

                1. MeinongsJungleBook on Chapter 17 Tue 12 May 2026 04:33PM AEST

                  Absolutely. “Lonely misfits finally encounter someone who gets them” is one of my all time favourite ship dynamics, which is part of why I glommed onto keero so hard. I think they both sort of see the Empire (maybe even Palpatine in a specifically parasocial way) as a substitute for the parents who failed them. And they’re both trying to prove that they’re better than their parents. Dedra tries to prove she’s not like her criminal parents by becoming an enforcer of the law. Syril tries to prove he’s not like his deadbeat dad by being as slavishly devoted as possible. It’s part of why I imagine Lonni sets them both off - they’re projecting their daddy issues onto him.

                    1. MeinongsJungleBook on Chapter 17 Wed 13 May 2026 10:16AM AEST

                      I imagine Dedra looks at Lonni and sees a man with a young child who is taking reckless risks that threaten his career and thus the child’s stability, like her own parents did which ended up with them in prison (presumably) and her in the kinderblock. And Syril sees a man shirking his duties to his wife and young child, like his own father did when he apparently flounced off and left him alone to Eedy’s tender mercies.

                      I could see Heert looking to Lonni for the validation, approval and guidance he never got from his father, much as Syril looks to Dedra for the validation and approval he didn’t get from his mother. What are fascists but bundles of Freudian issues?

                      1. marroniere_m on Chapter 17 Thu 14 May 2026 08:13AM AEST

                        This comment actually made me wonder about the differences in how Dedra and Syril see Lonni's behavior and how it's actually seen in the ISB (the code of conduct exists, but I believe Lonni has done some thorough risk assessment). I have a feeling Lonni understands that affairs between officers are seen as the permissible kind of violation. Partagaz has some history with Krennic which may or may not have been sexual. Lagret fucked someone's wife twenty years ago. Tarkin might have a reputation for fucking officers and troopers, if that stormtrooper short story is anything to go by. As long as you belong to the right circles and are discreet, nobody cares.

                        But that is, of course, not how the Empire works in Dedra's head.

                        I can absolutely see Heert seeking Lonni's validation, approval, and guidance. Probably even being comforted by Lonni when they get close enough to open up a little. Feeling safe and protected during sex — maybe because the communication is much better than whatever he's had before, and Lonni is very careful not to accidentally make him uncomfortable.

                        1. MeinongsJungleBook on Chapter 17 Thu 14 May 2026 09:15AM AEST

                          There is of course a chasm between what Syril believes the ISB to be and what it actually is. Dedra, I think, lives in a constant state of grinding cognitive dissonance between having a much clearer view than most of the corruption at the heart of the Empire, and her indoctrination telling her what she must unquestionably accept that the Empire is.

                          I assume the further you get up the ranks, the more you can get away with. I can imagine Krennic engaged in all sorts of depraved abuses of power that the Marquis de Sade would have been titillated to write about. At Supervisor level, you can probably get away with some things, but others are more iffy. I feel like the gay factor would make things more risky, although at the end of the day it’s a matter of how useful your superior thinks you are and how much they like you as to whether or not you actually get punished. Maybe that’s why Dedra thought she could get away with hoarding classified information with a slap on the wrist. If she’d just used her accumulated Permissible Bad Behaviour Points on sexually harassing her attendants instead she would have been fine 😔

                          Makes me wonder how Heert feels about it all after he finds out Lonni was a spy. Would he assume that all the tenderness and encouragement was nothing but a way to make him more pliable for manipulation?

                          1. marroniere_m on Chapter 17 Sun 17 May 2026 09:30AM AEST

                            Answering your questions about how Heert feels about it all after he finds out Lonni was a spy — I think that's such rich drama material!

                            He definitely must go back and forth. He must feel heartbroken, obviously, and betrayed, but he must also return to the moments he and Lonni shared over and over in his mind, looking for signs it was a rebel ploy that he should have spotted back then. And he will fail. Because it wasn't all a rebel ploy, and some part of him knows it, which makes him feel like he is going insane. It's just so hard to conceptualize for him.

                            I really love Jungheert everyone lives!AUs for the opportunities they give Heert to actually confront Lonni about this. I have a feeling that Lonni would initially be defensive — what do you think?

                            At some point, however, he would admit that it wasn't all a ploy.

                              It’s really interesting to think about how much Lonni would admit to Heert and to himself about what was real and what was just part of the facade.                                 I also have a feeling that the harder Lonni tries to save his marriage, the more he botches it.

                                If Lonni and Heert survive and have this conversation circa 1 BBY, it’s a mess. Just as you described here.
